Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A sophisticated group named HomeLand Justice targets healthcare organizations using expert-level skills to gain initial access through exploiting public-facing applications, execute malicious scripts, and impact operations by encrypting data for ransom.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Script File, Target Artifact (Key)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-30713 (Suggested): Apple macOS Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) contains an unspecified permissions issue which may allow a malicious application to bypass privacy preferences.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: The attackers exploit a known vulnerability in the healthcare system's internet-facing application to gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-1464 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows contains a spoofing vulnerability when Windows incorrectly validates file signatures, allowing an attacker to bypass security features and load improperly signed files.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: Using stolen credentials obtained during Initial Access, the attackers execute malicious scripts on internal systems to establish a foothold.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609; Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: The attackers deploy ransomware to encrypt critical data, rendering it inaccessible until a ransom is paid for the decryption key.

Scenario.summary: The HomeLand Justice group employs a straightforward yet effective approach to compromise healthcare systems. They exploit known vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain entry, execute malicious scripts to maintain control, and finally encrypt sensitive data to extort payment.
